Package microui.util

Class SpatialState

java.lang.Object
microui.util.SpatialState
public final class SpatialState extends Object
Represents a spatial state consisting of position (x, y) and dimensions (width, height). The state can be either fixed values or supplied dynamically via Suppliers. This class is primarily used by SpatialAnimator to animate transitions between different spatial configurations.
See Also:
  • SpatialView
  • SpatialAnimator

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    SpatialState()
    Constructs a SpatialState with all dimensions set to zero.
    SpatialState(float x, float y, float width, float height)
    Constructs a SpatialState with fixed position and dimensions.
    SpatialState(float x, float y, Supplier<Float> width, Supplier<Float> height)
    Constructs a SpatialState with fixed position and supplied dimensions.
    SpatialState(Supplier<Float> x, Supplier<Float> y, Supplier<Float> width, Supplier<Float> height)
    Constructs a SpatialState with all four dimensions supplied.
    SpatialState(SpatialView source)
    Constructs a SpatialState by copying the bounds from a given SpatialView.
    SpatialState(SpatialState source)
    Constructs a SpatialState by copying another SpatialState.

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    float
    getHeight()
    Returns the current height.
    float
    getWidth()
    Returns the current width.
    float
    getX()
    Returns the current x-coordinate.
    float
    getY()
    Returns the current y-coordinate.

    Sets a supplier for the height.

    Sets a supplier for the width.

    Sets a supplier for the X coordinate.

    setSupplierY(Supplier<Float> supplierY)
    Sets a supplier for the Y coordinate.

    Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

    equals, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

  • Constructor Details

    • SpatialState

      public SpatialState(float x, float y, float width, float height)
      Constructs a SpatialState with fixed position and dimensions.
      Parameters:
      x - the x-coordinate
      y - the y-coordinate
      width - the width
      height - the height

    • SpatialState

      public SpatialState(float x, float y, Supplier<Float> width, Supplier<Float> height)
      Constructs a SpatialState with fixed position and supplied dimensions.
      Parameters:
      x - the fixed x-coordinate
      y - the fixed y-coordinate
      width - the supplier for width (must not be null)
      height - the supplier for height (must not be null)
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if width or height is null

    • SpatialState

      public SpatialState(Supplier<Float> x, Supplier<Float> y, Supplier<Float> width, Supplier<Float> height)
      Constructs a SpatialState with all four dimensions supplied.
      Parameters:
      x - the supplier for x-coordinate (must not be null)
      y - the supplier for y-coordinate (must not be null)
      width - the supplier for width (must not be null)
      height - the supplier for height (must not be null)
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if any supplier is null

    • SpatialState

      public SpatialState(SpatialView source)
      Constructs a SpatialState by copying the bounds from a given SpatialView. The resulting state uses fixed values equal to the view's current position and size.
      Parameters:
      source - the SpatialView to copy from (must not be null)
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if source is null

    • SpatialState

      public SpatialState(SpatialState source)
      Constructs a SpatialState by copying another SpatialState. The resulting state uses fixed values equal to the source's current values (any suppliers in the source are ignored, only the actual values are copied).
      Parameters:
      source - the SpatialState to copy from (must not be null)
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if source is null

    • SpatialState

      public SpatialState()
      Constructs a SpatialState with all dimensions set to zero.

  • Method Details

    • setSupplierX

      public void setSupplierX(Supplier<Float> supplierX)
      Sets a supplier for the X coordinate. When present, getX() will use this supplier.
      Parameters:
      supplierX - the supplier for X, must not be null
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if supplierX is null

    • setSupplierY

      public void setSupplierY(Supplier<Float> supplierY)
      Sets a supplier for the Y coordinate. When present, getY() will use this supplier.
      Parameters:
      supplierY - the supplier for Y, must not be null
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if supplierY is null

    • setSupplierWidth

      public void setSupplierWidth(Supplier<Float> supplierWidth)
      Sets a supplier for the width. When present, getWidth() will use this supplier.
      Parameters:
      supplierWidth - the supplier for width, must not be null
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if supplierWidth is null

    • setSupplierHeight

      public void setSupplierHeight(Supplier<Float> supplierHeight)
      Sets a supplier for the height. When present, getHeight() will use this supplier.
      Parameters:
      supplierHeight - the supplier for height, must not be null
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if supplierHeight is null

    • getX

      public float getX()
      Returns the current x-coordinate. If a supplier is set, its value is returned; otherwise the fixed value given at construction is returned.
      Returns:
      the current x-coordinate

    • getY

      public float getY()
      Returns the current y-coordinate. If a supplier is set, its value is returned; otherwise the fixed value given at construction is returned.
      Returns:
      the current y-coordinate

    • getWidth

      public float getWidth()
      Returns the current width. If a supplier is set, its value is returned; otherwise the fixed value given at construction is returned.
      Returns:
      the current width

    • getHeight

      public float getHeight()
      Returns the current height. If a supplier is set, its value is returned; otherwise the fixed value given at construction is returned.
      Returns:
      the current height
